ddls:
CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "dept_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "dept_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "dept_no" INTEGER /* Department number */,
  "mgr_ssn" INTEGER /* SSN of the manager */,
  "mgr_start_date" TEXT /* Date when the manager started */,
  "parent_dept_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent department */,
  "dept_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the department */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dept_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "emp_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "emp_name" TEXT /* Full name of the employee */,
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job title */,
  "dept_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the employee belongs to */,
  "mgr_ssn" INTEGER /* SSN of the manager */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date when the employee was hired */,
  "salary" TEXT /* Salary of the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("emp_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_mgr_ssn FOREIGN KEY ("mgr_ssn") REFERENCES employees ("emp_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_dept_id FOREIGN KEY ("dept_id") REFERENCES departments ("dept_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_title_id") REFERENCES job_titles ("job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_titles" (
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Name of the job title */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job title */,
  "min_salary" TEXT /* Minimum salary for the job title */,
  "max_salary" TEXT /* Maximum salary for the job title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dept_history" (
  "hist_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical change */,
  "dept_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department affected */,
  "emp_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee affected */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change occurred */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., promotion, transfer) */,
  "previous_dept_id" INTEGER /* Previous department ID for employee transfers */,
  "previous_job_title_id" INTEGER /* Previous job title ID for employee promotions or transfers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hist_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dept_history_previous_dept_id FOREIGN KEY ("previous_dept_id") REFERENCES departments ("dept_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dept_history_emp_id FOREIGN KEY ("emp_id") REFERENCES employees ("emp_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dept_history_dept_id FOREIGN KEY ("dept_id") REFERENCES departments ("dept_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dept_history_previous_job_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("previous_job_title_id") REFERENCES job_titles ("job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "role_permissions" TEXT /* Permissions assigned to the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_roles" (
  "emp_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("emp_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "department_managers" (
  "dept_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department */,
  "emp_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the manager */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dept_id", "emp_id")
)
column_names: Reference to the department the employee belongs to
Date when the employee was hired
Date of birth of the employee
Unique identifier for each job title
Minimum salary for the job title
Maximum salary for the job title
Unique identifier for each historical change
Previous department ID for employee transfers
Previous job title ID for employee promotions or transfers
